instruction: How should a freelance photographer set up their business in TurboTax for maximum deductions?
output: Use TurboTax Self-Employed or Premium. On Schedule C, set the principal business code to 541920 (Photographic Services) and business type as Sole Proprietorship (unless they have an LLC). This unlocks all the self-employment deductions including home office, vehicle, equipment, and the QBI deduction.

instruction: What equipment can a photographer deduct on taxes?
output: Camera bodies, lenses, flashes, tripods, drones, memory cards, batteries, camera bags, computers, color-calibrated monitors, external drives, editing software (Lightroom, Photoshop, Capture One), printers, paper, and ink. Use Section 179 to deduct the FULL cost of equipment in the year purchased instead of depreciating over several years.

instruction: What software and subscription costs can a photographer write off?
output: Adobe Creative Cloud, cloud storage (Google Drive, Dropbox, Backblaze), website hosting (Squarespace, Wix, WordPress), booking/CRM tools (HoneyBook, Dubsado, Studio Ninja), accounting software (QuickBooks, Wave), and music licensing for video work. All are fully deductible as business expenses.

instruction: How does the mileage deduction work for photographers?
output: Track all driving to shoots, scouting locations, gear pickups, client meetings, and equipment stores. The standard mileage rate is 67 cents/mile for 2024 and 70 cents/mile for 2025. Even 10,000 miles at 70 cents equals a $7,000 deduction. Use Google Maps Timeline or a mileage tracking app to reconstruct your log. Also deduct parking and tolls at shoot locations.

instruction: Can a photographer deduct travel expenses?
output: Yes. Flights, hotels, rental cars for destination shoots are deductible. Meals while traveling for shoots are 50% deductible. Mileage for local driving to shoots, consultations, and gear stores is deductible at the standard rate (67 cents/mile 2024, 70 cents/mile 2025). Parking and tolls at shoot locations are also deductible.

instruction: What marketing expenses can a photographer deduct?
output: Website domain and hosting, business cards, flyers, portfolio prints, social media ads (Instagram, Facebook), SEO services, and sample albums for client meetings. All are fully deductible business expenses on Schedule C.

instruction: How does the home office deduction work for photographers?
output: If you have a dedicated space at home for editing, you qualify for the home office deduction. TurboTax offers a simplified method: $5 per square foot up to 300 sq ft, for a maximum $1,500 deduction. The actual expenses method lets you deduct the percentage of rent/mortgage, utilities, and internet proportional to your office square footage vs total home square footage. A separate rented studio is fully deductible.

instruction: What insurance and professional fees can photographers deduct?
output: Gear insurance, liability insurance, professional memberships (PPA, ASMP), workshop and conference registration fees, and education courses (online photography classes, business courses). All are deductible on Schedule C.

instruction: What commonly overlooked deductions do photographers miss?
output: Phone bill (business use percentage), internet (same percentage split), props/backdrops/styling items for shoots, model fees and assistant pay (1099 them if over $600), second shooting fees paid to other photographers, albums/prints/USB drives delivered to clients, and shipping costs. The biggest money most photographers leave on the table is mileage and Section 179 equipment deductions.

instruction: What is the QBI deduction and how does it help photographers?
output: The Qualified Business Income (QBI) deduction gives a 20% deduction on net business income if total taxable income is under approximately $191,000 for single filers or $383,000 for married filing jointly. This is a significant tax benefit for self-employed photographers that TurboTax will calculate automatically on Schedule C income.

instruction: What should a photographer have ready before doing taxes in TurboTax?
output: All bank and credit card statements for the year, a mileage log (Google Maps Timeline helps reconstruct one), a list of all gear purchased with dates and amounts, any 1099s received from clients, home office square footage and total home square footage, and the previous year's tax return (TurboTax can import it).

instruction: How does self-employment tax work for photographers?
output: Freelance photographers owe approximately 15.3% self-employment tax on their net profit (12.4% Social Security + 2.9% Medicare). However, half of the SE tax is deductible on the front of your return (Form 1040), reducing your adjusted gross income. If you owe over $1,000 at tax time, you should be making quarterly estimated tax payments to avoid underpayment penalties.
